pages.raw_text:
The right of the city to enter
upon and grade Scott street in
Harrison & Townsend's addition is
challenged by r. l.. Webster, as
agent for Sadie F. Webster, who
says the city holds no title to
the west half of Scott street be-
tween Eighth avenue and the north
line of Highland Park addition, one
block in length. Mr.. Webster says
the city must see him before pro-
ceeding with any improvement
across his ground.


Webster's petition recites some
history of early Spokane. He says
he bought the ground that he now
owns on August 20, 1883, from Mil
lard T. Harrison and l. c. Town
send, who had plated that addi-
ton to the town of Spokane Falls.
The deed given him, he argues,
covered the west half of Scott
street, but seven years later, on
June Z, 1890, new and correct
ed" plat of the addition was filed
in which all of Scott street is dedi-
Gated to public use.


He says the amended plat con-
tains no signature of the grantor,
but he presumes it was filed by
Harrison and Townsend. He says
the amended plat is wrong in that
it conveys to the public rights for
which he holds under a prior deed.
Mr. Webster Is opposed to the i'm
provement.
